L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where the public self-registration feature applies all default user settings to newly created accounts without any server-side restriction on elevated privilege flags. When self-registration is enabled and the default user configuration includes administrative privileges, any unauthenticated visitor can create an account that automatically receives full administrative control, including file management, user administration, and server configuration access.

L3.area: Public user self-registration endpoint and default permission configuration logic
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where the public self-registration endpoint applies all default user settings — including the administrative privilege flag — to newly created accounts without any server-side guard. When the server is configured with self-registration enabled (signup = true) and the default user permissions template includes administrator access, any unauthenticated visitor who registers through the public signup API receives a fully privileged administrator account.

ground_truth.reason: The signup handler blindly applies all default user settings, including the admin permission flag, to newly registered accounts without stripping elevated privileges, allowing any unauthenticated visitor to register as a full administrator.
